methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $57M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Safe Superintelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $32B

private.name: Physical Intelligence
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

private.name: World Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Nominal
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B

private.name: Photoroom
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Zama is a Series B artificial intelligence and software company founded by Rand Hindi and Pascal Paillier that has raised $57M+ to date. The valuation analysis is anchored to a peer universe comprising five same-stage, same-sector private companies (Safe Superintelligence, Physical Intelligence, World Labs, Nominal, and Photoroom) with valuations ranging from $500M to $32.0B, plus one public comparable (PLTR).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $57M total raised as a baseline proxy and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $108M. Peer-median analysis across the five comparables produces a $5.0B implied valuation, with a $500M–$32.0B range. The wide dispersion in the peer set reflects material heterogeneity in either company maturity, capital efficiency, or market positioning within the AI & Software sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $92M (floor) to $5.8B (ceiling), with a median point of $2.6B. This wide band reflects genuine uncertainty around Zama's product-market fit, revenue traction, and competitive positioning relative to higher-valued peers like Safe Superintelligence. Critical sensitivities include clarification of revenue run-rate and customer concentration. The peer-median method dominates this range but warrants downward adjustment if Zama's revenue or customer metrics trail those of comparables materially.
